entries.text: A man of Nagasaki, a Japanese Gulliver, 374–376; story adapted from Professor B. H. Chamberlain's translation in the Transactions of the Asiatic Society of Japan, 374; arrives at Sea of Mud, 375; meets Jofuku, 375; starts on journey to the Three Thousand Worlds mentioned in Buddhist Scriptures, 375; visits Lands of Endless Plenty, of Shams, of the Followers of the Antique, of Paradoxes, and of Giants, 375
Finds out all details of Kintaro's life, 368
His description of the lotus flower, 169
Story adapted from Mr. R. Gordon Smith's Ancient Tales and Folklore of Japan, 177; Heitaro, husband of Higo, the, 178–180
The, in Japanese art, 112, 113; the Mountain, 355
Mentioned in Buddhist Scriptures, 375; Shikaiya Wasōbiyōye journeys to, 375
Legendary origin of the Chinese system of, 363
